CheckTitle: AI Agent Execution Logs Must Be Complete
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Observability**. Verifies that AI agent execution logs capture all actions, tool invocations, decisions, and outputs. Incomplete execution logs prevent post-incident forensics and make it impossible to reconstruct what an agent did during a security incident.
Risk: Without complete execution logs, attackers can cause significant harm through an agent and leave no forensic trail. Security teams cannot determine the scope of a breach, which actions were taken, or which data was accessed during an incident.

Code.Other:
1. Configure structured logging for all agent actions, tool calls, LLM inputs/outputs, and decisions.
2. Ship logs to a centralised SIEM or log management platform.
3. Ensure log format includes agent ID, timestamp, action type, inputs, outputs, and status.
4. Validate log completeness with automated checks before each deployment.

Recommendation.Text: Implement structured, complete execution logging for all agent operations. Include tool invocations, decision points, LLM inputs and outputs, and error conditions. Ship logs to a centralised platform with tamper detection.

Notes: ASPM-077 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
